pr.title: replit.md: add deploy-sync policy so Agent fetches origin before reporting
pr.body:
## Summary
- Last incident: Replit Agent said "nothing to pull" while `origin/main` was 5 commits ahead, because it answered from its diverged local HEAD without `git fetch` first. The two local-ahead commits were just Replit auto-checkpoints (buildNumber bump + mockup imports) — harmless on their own, but they hid the real deploy state.
- Add a User Preferences entry to `replit.md` declaring `origin/main` as source of truth and codifying the pre-deploy fetch + branch on what's local-ahead.
- Safe by default: `git reset --hard` only runs when every local commit carries the `Replit-Commit-Author:` trailer (the auto-checkpoint signature). Any hand-authored commit or uncommitted change halts and asks the user — avoids nuking work if you ever do edit in Replit by chance.

## Test plan
- [ ] Next time Replit Agent is asked "what's deployable" / "pull from main", confirm it runs `git fetch origin` and reports incoming commits rather than the local-view answer.
- [ ] Verify it discards Replit auto-checkpoints automatically but stops on real local commits.


---
